name: Vegetable Consomme with Mushroom Ravioli
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion, halved and thinly sliced
9 oz leek, well washed, white and tender green part sliced
8 oz carrots, pared and thinly sliced
6 oz parsnips, pared and thinly sliced
4 oz turnip, pared and thinly sliced
13 1/3 oz can plum tomatoes in juice
1 leek, washed well and finely diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Broth: In a large saucepan, sauté onion, leek, garlic, and ginger in 2 tbsp olive oil over medium heat until softened, about 10 minutes.
2. Add remaining olive oil, carrot, parsnip, and turnip; cook for 5 minutes.
3. Add tomatoes with juice, salt, marjoram, and water. Bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 45 minutes. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing on solids to extract all liquid. Discard solids. Set aside.
5. Ravioli: In a skillet, sauté shallots in olive oil over medium-low heat until softened, about 6 minutes.
6. Add mushrooms; cook for 10 minutes until mushrooms are soft and almost all liquid has evaporated.
7. Stir in salt, pepper, and port wine. Cook for 5 minutes until liquid has evaporated.
8. Cool to room temperature. Lay 12 wonton wrappers on a dry work surface.
9. Cut each wonton in half lengthwise. Place a spoonful of mushroom mixture on the top half of each wonton.
10. Dampen the four sides of the top half with water. Fold the dry half over the filling and press down to seal. Repeat with remaining wrappers and filling.
11. Bring consomme to a boil in a large saucepan. Drop in ravioli; cook for 2 minutes.
12. Add diced carrot, parsnip, and leek; cook for an additional 2 minutes.
13. Serve.
